verses.text: Thou that judgest doest the same things; canst thou escape God's judgment? Self-condemned. To judge others while sinning is to condemn oneself.
Thou that judgest doest the same things; canst thou escape God's judgment? Self-condemned. To judge others while sinning is to condemn oneself.
Thou that judgest doest the same things; canst thou escape God's judgment? Self-condemned. To judge others while sinning is to condemn oneself.
His goodness 'leadeth thee to repentance'; the hard heart 'treasurest up... wrath.' Despised mercy. Patience scorned only deepens the coming judgment.
His goodness 'leadeth thee to repentance'; the hard heart 'treasurest up... wrath.' Despised mercy. Patience scorned only deepens the coming judgment.
God will 'render to every man according to his deeds,' life to the patient, wrath to the rebellious. Just reckoning. God judges by what men do, not say.
God will 'render to every man according to his deeds,' life to the patient, wrath to the rebellious. Just reckoning. God judges by what men do, not say.
God will 'render to every man according to his deeds,' life to the patient, wrath to the rebellious. Just reckoning. God judges by what men do, not say.
Tribulation on the evildoer, glory on the well-doer; 'there is no respect of persons with God.' Impartial. Jew and Gentile are judged alike.
Tribulation on the evildoer, glory on the well-doer; 'there is no respect of persons with God.' Impartial. Jew and Gentile are judged alike.
Tribulation on the evildoer, glory on the well-doer; 'there is no respect of persons with God.' Impartial. Jew and Gentile are judged alike.
'Not the hearers of the law... but the doers... shall be justified.' Doing, not hearing. To know the law is not to keep it.
'Not the hearers of the law... but the doers... shall be justified.' Doing, not hearing. To know the law is not to keep it.
The Gentiles, doing by nature the law's things, show 'the work of the law written in their hearts.' Conscience within. God's law is known even apart from Moses.
The Gentiles, doing by nature the law's things, show 'the work of the law written in their hearts.' Conscience within. God's law is known even apart from Moses.
The Gentiles, doing by nature the law's things, show 'the work of the law written in their hearts.' Conscience within. God's law is known even apart from Moses.
Thou restest in the law and boastest of God, a guide of the blind. Proud privilege. They trusted their advantages instead of obeying.
Thou restest in the law and boastest of God, a guide of the blind. Proud privilege. They trusted their advantages instead of obeying.
Thou restest in the law and boastest of God, a guide of the blind. Proud privilege. They trusted their advantages instead of obeying.
Thou restest in the law and boastest of God, a guide of the blind. Proud privilege. They trusted their advantages instead of obeying.
Thou that teachest another, teachest thou not thyself? 'The name of God is blasphemed... through you.' Hypocrisy. Their sin dishonoured the God they preached.
Thou that teachest another, teachest thou not thyself? 'The name of God is blasphemed... through you.' Hypocrisy. Their sin dishonoured the God they preached.

Circumcision profits if thou keep the law; the obedient uncircumcised shall judge thee. Heart, not sign. The mark avails nothing without obedience.
Circumcision profits if thou keep the law; the obedient uncircumcised shall judge thee. Heart, not sign. The mark avails nothing without obedience.
Circumcision profits if thou keep the law; the obedient uncircumcised shall judge thee. Heart, not sign. The mark avails nothing without obedience.
He is a Jew 'which is one inwardly'; circumcision is 'of the heart, in the spirit.' True religion. God seeks the inward, not the outward, mark.
